info.description: Plateforme de déploiement autonome pour Docker Swarm.
info.title: Hivemind API

post.description: Returns an access token (15 min) and a refresh token (7 days).
Stateless JWT: the client discards both tokens. A server-side blocklist will be added post-MVP.

get.description: Returns id, email and role of the user owning the access token.

post.description: The old refresh token is implicitly invalidated on the client side (stateless MVP).
post.summary: Exchange a refresh token for a new token pair

post.description: Stores a cleartext config file (UTF-8, max 500 KB) as version 1.

422.description: invalid name, content too large or invalid UTF-8

get.description: Returns config metadata (no content — use the versions endpoint to read content).

delete.description: Fails if the config is still attached to any service.

get.description: Returns the full version history with content, newest first.

post.description: Stores new content as the next version. Attached services pick it up on their next deployment.

422.description: content too large or invalid UTF-8

get.description: Global deployment history, filterable by service, status and time range (RFC3339), newest first.

parameters.description: Filter by status (pending | in_progress | succeeded | failed | rolled_back)
parameters.name: status

post.description: Registers an overlay network definition. It is materialised on Swarm when a service using it is deployed.

delete.description: Fails if the network is still attached to any service.

get.description: Returns secret metadata only — values are never exposed.

post.description: Stores a write-only secret. The value is encrypted at rest and never returned by any endpoint.

delete.description: Fails if the secret is still attached to any service.

post.description: Stores a new encrypted version and increments the version counter. Attached services pick up the new version on their next deployment.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of services. Filter by name (partial, case-insensitive) and/or status.

post.description: Saves a new service in draft status. Does NOT deploy it — use POST /services/{id}/deploy for that.

422.description: invalid name format or resource constraint

get.description: Returns the full service definition and its current status.

put.description: Updates mutable service fields. The service name is immutable. Only non-null fields are changed.

delete.description: Deletes a draft or removed service. Deleting a deployed service requires the deployment engine (F-MVP-08) to be active; until then, undeploy the service first.

get.summary: List configs attached to a service

post.summary: Attach a config to a service

delete.summary: Detach a config from a service

post.description: Triggers a deployment of the service to the orchestrator. Runs asynchronously: responds 202 with a pending deployment; poll GET /deployments/{id} for the outcome.

409.description: a deployment is already in progress

get.description: Returns the env vars of a service. Secret values are masked (empty string) and never returned in clear text.

put.description: Atomically replaces the full set of env vars (F-MVP-04). The submitted list is authoritative — omitted keys are removed. Keys must match ^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$ and be unique. Secret values are encrypted at rest.

get.description: Upgrades to a WebSocket and attaches an interactive TTY exec session to one of the service's containers. Client→server text messages are tagged by a leading byte: '0'=stdin data, '1'=JSON resize {cols,rows}. Server→client messages are raw terminal output. Authenticate with ?token=<access_token> (WebSocket cannot carry an Authorization header). Requires the Admin role.

Streams the service's aggregated container logs as Server-Sent Events. Each log line is delivered as a `data:` event; an `event: end` frame closes a non-follow stream. With follow=true the stream stays open until the client disconnects. Authenticate with a Bearer token (use fetch/ReadableStream, not EventSource).

parameters.description: Prefix each line with an RFC3339 timestamp
parameters.name: timestamps

parameters.description: Only logs since this time (RFC3339 or duration like 10m)
parameters.name: since

get.summary: List networks attached to a service

post.summary: Attach a network to a service

delete.summary: Detach a network from a service

put.description: Updates only the resource reservations and limits (F-MVP-03). CPU is in decimal cores, memory in bytes. A limit of 0 means "unbounded"; a non-zero limit must be >= its reservation.

422.description: limit below reservation or negative value

get.summary: List secrets attached to a service

post.summary: Attach a secret to a service

delete.summary: Detach a secret from a service

get.description: Returns the aggregated orchestrator view of a service: effective vs. desired replicas (running x/y), plus pending/failed counts and whether an update is in progress. Read in near real time from Swarm with a short cache (TTL <= 5s).

Returns the per-task (container) detail of a service: executing node, current and desired state, last update and any Swarm error message. Read in near real time from Swarm with a short cache (TTL <= 5s).
